title: Colorama chaotic-scramble sweep on solid source

metricComparisons.description: amount=1 should scramble per-pixel hue across the solid red field, raising the live spatial stddev across RGB channels (deterministic per-pixel from UV hash)

expectedVideo: amount=0 should be inaudibly identical to bypass (uniform red field)
amount=0.5 should show partial per-pixel hue dispersion — a noisy but still red-leaning field
amount=1 should fully scramble per-pixel hue (high spatialStd across RGB channels)
expectedAudio: amount=0 should be inaudibly identical to bypass
amount=0.5 should add audible ring-mod sidebands without dominating the dry signal
amount=1 should be fully wet ring-mod with a chaotically swept 50–350 Hz carrier

Audio side is MANUAL REVIEW ONLY (no hard gate): the solid source's exported-WAV baseline spectral metrics are not stable enough across fresh browser sessions to hard-gate. Precedent: audit-modulate-osc-sweep / audit-scrollX-osc-sweep. The .webm captures the full sweep for listening.
manualChecks: Does the scramble read as colour chaos rather than visible per-frame noise / banding?
Is the ring-mod musically usable at intermediate amounts, or does it sound like a fault?
Do visual chaos depth and audible ring-mod depth track together as the slider moves?
